name: Ankr Decentralized Web3 Infrastructure API
what_it_does: Decentralized blockchain infrastructure platform with REST and RPC APIs supporting 70+ blockchains — providing fast RPC nodes, NFT API, DeFi data APIs, and staking infrastructure with both free shared nodes and premium dedicated nodes.
use_cases: Querying NFT metadata, ownership, and transfer history across 10+ chains via unified API
Accessing fast EVM RPC endpoints for smart contract interactions
Fetching DeFi token prices and liquidity data across multiple DEXs
Running on-chain queries across 70+ blockchains from a single API key
Building multi-chain wallet applications with unified balance and transaction APIs
not_for: Single-chain development where provider-specific features matter (use Alchemy or Infura)
Enterprise SLA requirements without dedicated node plan
Compliance-focused blockchain analytics (use Chainalysis or Elliptic)
best_when: An agent needs to interact with many different blockchains from a single API provider with competitive pricing, especially for NFT and DeFi data.
avoid_when: You need premium developer experience for single chain — Alchemy or QuickNode have better tooling.

auth.notes: API key from Ankr console. Passed as query parameter or header depending on endpoint. Free public RPC endpoints available without key. Premium endpoints require key.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: shared RPC nodes with rate limiting; 30M compute units/month
pricing.paid_tiers: Premium from $5/month (extra compute units); Dedicated nodes from $199/month
pricing.notes: Compute unit-based pricing. Very competitive free tier for development. Premium dedicated nodes for production. ANKR token can be used for payment. Popular for multi-chain development.

performance.rate_limits: Compute units per plan; shared nodes have higher latency than dedicated

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API (Advanced API) and RPC documentation. NFT API and DeFi API well-documented. Error responses for API calls include codes. RPC errors follow JSON-RPC spec.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All blockchain data reads are idempotent. RPC calls for the same block are deterministic. NFT and DeFi data queries are stateless reads.
known_agent_gotchas: Free shared nodes have rate limits and higher latency than dedicated — not for production
Compute unit consumption varies by endpoint type — monitor usage to avoid overages
Some chains have less robust coverage than others — verify per-chain data completeness
Advanced API (REST) and RPC endpoints have different auth models

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key auth (query param on some endpoints — less secure). No formal compliance certifications. Public RPC endpoints without auth available. Blockchain data is public.
